title: Scope Discovery Manifest
description: Per-feature scope-manifest.yaml. Generated as a strawman by `dw-lifecycle scope-inventory` and curated by the operator. Drives subsequent multi-agent discovery, UI Playwright walks, AST/clone audits, and the dispatch wrapper's sibling-enumeration gate. Lives at docs/<version>/<status>/<feature-slug>/scope-manifest.yaml.

kind.description: Which discovery mode applies. ui: Playwright route walk + DOM-token snapshot. code: AST + clone-detector matrix across module file globs. hybrid: union of both, deduplicated by file-path key.

feature_slug.description: The feature directory name under docs/<version>/<status>/. Used to anchor evidence paths and journal entries.

version.description: Optional schema-revision tag for the manifest itself. Bumped when the manifest format changes in a backwards-incompatible way.

generated_by.description: How the manifest was produced. Strawman: machine-generated by /scope-inventory awaiting operator curation. Curated: operator has reviewed and pruned. Hand-authored: operator wrote from scratch (rare; flag for review).

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of last machine generation (set by /scope-inventory). Curation does not update this field; the journal entry is the curation record.

scenarios.description: Named scenarios under which surfaces are exercised. For ui kind these are typically dev-server query parameters (?midi=simulated&scenario=X); for code kind these are pattern-shape labels. Must contain at least one entry.

reference_docs.description: Authoritative documents the discovery and synthesis passes treat as ground truth: PRD, workplan, mockups, design-language references, analysis reports. Discovery-agent findings that contradict reference docs are flagged in the synthesis pass, not silently overridden.

discovery_themes.description: Pattern shapes the PRD-themed discovery agents are seeded with. For a redesign feature: things like 'cross-page chrome consistency', 'list-bank chevron sizing'. For a typing-cleanup feature: 'as Type casts', 'any escapes'. Free-text labels — the agents interpret them.

routes.description: UI routes to walk. Required when kind is ui or hybrid; forbidden when kind is code.

modules.description: Source-code module entries to audit. Required when kind is code or hybrid; forbidden when kind is ui.

regime_holdouts.description: Optional regime-holdout section emitted by the T6.5 regime-holdout-detector agent. Fans the agent's findings into four per-source buckets (anti-pattern matches, adopter-manifest holdouts, editor-symmetry partial/missing cells, deprecation-queue importers) plus a meta summary. Absent when the agent did not contribute findings (existing pre-T6.5 manifests still validate).

anti_patterns.description: Findings sourced from the T6.1 anti-pattern registry scan.

adopter_manifests.description: Findings sourced from the T6.2 adopter-manifest registry scan.

editor_symmetry.description: Findings sourced from the T6.3 cross-editor symmetry matrix (partial/missing cells).

deprecations.description: Findings sourced from the T6.4 deprecation scan (one entry per importer of a @deprecated file).

meta.description: Per-source counts + total. Useful for at-a-glance burndown without iterating the per-bucket arrays. Phase 11 Task 11 adds `by_status` rollup so operators can see actively-enforced vs. candidate counts at-a-glance.

by_status.description: Phase 11 Task 11 — per-status rollup. `actively_enforced` are findings sourced from `blessed`/`cursed` catalog entries (gate-blocking). `candidate` are findings from `pending` entries (operator-triage surface; non-gate-blocking). Suppressed statuses (ignore / tracked-holdout / withdrawn) never surface — they are filtered upstream at the scanner level.

codebase_state_metrics.description: Phase 11 Task 4 — codebase-state metrics computed during the synthesis pass. Informational: feeds the Phase 11 Task 5 self-correcting controller and operator-visible dashboards. Optional at the manifest level (legacy manifests omit it).

notes.description: Free-text operator notes attached to the manifest. Not consumed by tooling.

$comment: ajv strict mode warns about 'required' inside conditional branches without sibling 'properties' declarations. The runtime semantics are correct (verified by the example fixtures exercised in validate.ts); only the lint complains. Validators wishing to use strict mode should set strictRequired: false.

id.description: Scenario identifier — referenced by route entries (and, indirectly, by discovery-agent findings).

label.description: Human-readable scenario label, shown in evidence captures.

description.description: What the scenario exercises — which device state, which fixtures, etc.

query.description: Query-string fragment appended to ui routes (e.g., 'midi=simulated&scenario=tones-bank-0').

path.description: Repo-relative path to the reference document.

role.description: Why the discovery layer should consult this document.

summary.description: One-line summary of what the document contributes — helps discovery agents avoid re-deriving.

path.description: URL path of the route, leading slash. E.g., '/patches', '/roland/s550/editor/play'.

devices.description: Device identifiers the route should be walked under. Multi-device editors capture once per device. Use ['none'] for device-agnostic routes.

scenarios.description: Scenario ids (must reference the manifest-level scenarios[].id list). Discovery walks the cross-product (route x device x scenario).

primitives.description: Cross-page primitives the route is expected to render. Used by the synthesis pass to flag drifted consumers.

skip_reason.description: If set, the route is enumerated for completeness but skipped at capture time with this reason recorded in the journal. Reasons must be specific and non-deferral (no 'TODO', 'for now', 'later' phrasings).

glob.description: Source file glob (relative to repo root) for the module's AST/clone audit. E.g., 'modules/roland-sxx0-editor/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'.

relevance.description: Optional PRD-derived relevance score (AUDIT-20260524-11). Emitted by the strawman synthesizer when the PRD's 'In Scope' / 'Out of Scope' sections name the module. 'low' marks a module the operator should review for actual relevance; 'high' / 'medium' are informational. 'excluded' modules are filtered out of the manifest BEFORE emission and never appear here.

patterns.description: Pattern/AST shapes being inventoried in this module. Each entry names what discovery agents are looking for; the synthesis pass groups findings by pattern id.

excludes.description: Globs intentionally excluded from this module's audit, each paired with a specific non-deferral reason. Pairing glob+reason in a single object lets the schema enforce that every exclusion carries a justification (the previous parallel-array shape could not).

glob.description: File glob (relative to repo root) excluded from this module's audit.

reason.description: Why this glob is excluded. Reasons must be specific and non-deferral (no 'TODO', 'for now', 'later' phrasings).

id.description: Pattern identifier — discovery findings reference this id.

kind.description: What shape of pattern this is. ast-call: function/method call shape. ast-jsx: JSX element/prop shape. type-cast: 'as Type' or '<Type>' assertions. grep: literal string or regex match. clone-group: code-clone detector group. import: import-specifier shape.

description.description: What discovery agents should look for and why it matters.

query.description: Concrete query — regex, AST selector, or jscpd group id. Format depends on the pattern's kind; the agent interpreting it knows the convention.

concentration.description: Gini-style concentration on [0,1]; null when total_hits < 2.

regime_holdout_entry.description: One regime-holdout finding. Emitted by the T6.5 regime-holdout-detector and grouped by source bucket in the manifest's regime_holdouts section. Phase 11 Task 11 adds `status_provenance` so each finding traces back to the catalog entry's status + provenance.

id.description: Identifier within the source bucket. Anti-pattern id, adopter-manifest id, '<manifest-id>:<editor>' composite, or path of the deprecated file.

file.description: Repo-relative POSIX path of the offending file (or editor module dir for editor-symmetry rows).

line.description: Optional 1-based source line. Omitted for whole-file findings (e.g., adopter-manifest holdouts, editor-symmetry rows).

shape.description: Human description of the legacy / missing / drifted shape the gate caught.

replacement.description: Human description of the canonical replacement the operator should apply.

evidence.description: Back-pointer to the registry / scan output that caught the holdout. Lets a reader resolve the manifest entry back to its source registry in one hop.

registry_path.description: Repo-relative path of the registry YAML or scan-output that caught the holdout.

registry_id.description: Stable identifier within the registry (anti-pattern id, manifest id, '@deprecated' marker, etc.).

status_provenance.description: Phase 11 Task 11 — inherited status + provenance from the catalog entry that produced the finding. `source_status` is the catalog `status:` literal (pending / blessed / cursed / ignore / tracked-holdout / withdrawn). `provenance_source` is the catalog `provenance.source` literal. Deprecation findings synthesize `blessed` + `install-seed` (the marker IS the registry; there is no Loop field).
